items.title: A welcome enhancement by HSBC! 🎉 The HSBC Rewards Portal, including Hopper for ✈️ flight and 🏨 hotel bookings, is now available on Desktop/Laptop as well. 💻 Here’s how to get started: 👉 Log in to the HSBC Mobile App and update your mobile number & email ID: HSBC App → Credit Card → View More → Reward Points → Redeem Points Now → Profile Icon → My Account → My Profile 👉 Open the HSBC Rewards Portal on your Desktop/Laptop: https://rewards.hsbc.co.in 👉 Click “Forgot Password” to set/reset your password. (Your User ID is your registered email ID.) 👉 Log in to the Desktop Rewards Portal using your registered email ID and the newly created password. Browsing, comparing and booking flights & hotels is now much more convenient on a larger screen. 👍 Thanks @SwipesAndSmiles for sharing this. #creditcard #spendwisely #creditcard

items.title: 🇮🇳 Indians can continue to enter Thailand🇹🇭 without a visa and without paying any visa-related fee 👏👏👏 Earlier, in May, the Thai government had approved a proposal to scrap visa-free entry for Indians. However, it was never implemented. Now, they have decided not to implement the proposal, considering the drop in Indian tourist arrivals over the last couple of months. 🇹🇭✈️ Thailand continues to be one of the most accessible and budget-friendly international destinations, offering: 🏖️ Stunning beaches 🌃 Vibrant nightlife 🍜 Amazing food 🏨 Excellent Accor & Marriott hotels across every budget, making it a fantastic destination for both cash stays and points redemptions. items.title: Imagine checking your credit card app and realizing you’ve built a mini gold reserve just by living your life. 🪙✨ Most cards reward you with loyalty points that expire before you can ever use them. But the Uni GoldX Card does something entirely different—it turns your everyday expenses into real, 24K Digital Gold. Best of all? It’s Lifetime Free with zero joining or annual fees. 💳 Here is how that looks in real life: ✈️ The Vacation Win You book, for example, ₹1 Lakh worth of flights/hotels for a family trip through the Uni App. Instead of just getting air miles, you instantly get 7% back—that’s ₹7,000 worth of actual digital gold added to your stash, with no upper limit. Heading abroad? Enjoy Zero Forex Markup across 180+ countries. So easy savings of 5K-10K again. 🌍 🛍️ The Daily Grind Whether you're shopping on Amazon, Flipkart, Swiggy, Myntra or any other popular app, GoldX gives you straight 5% back via vouchers on Uni Store. Scanning a UPI QR code at a local café, paying insurance, or buying gold, you earn 1% back in Uni Coins (100 coins = Gold worth ₹1 ). 🎬 The Weekend Reset Spend ₹10,000+ a month on the card, and your next movie night at PVR/INOX is 50% off (up to ₹250). Get this benefit every month. ✂️Pay 1/3rd You can also split all flight fares and gift vouchers into 3 parts. This Zero Cost EMI feature can be super handy to ease out the expenses. 💰 The Payoff You aren't stuck trying to redeem points for a random trip. You can actually cash out your digital gold at prevailing market prices, or choose to mint it into physical gold coins. Zero Making Charges 🥇 ⚠️ The Fine Print It’s issued by YES BANK and BOBCARD. You get 2 variants - physical card and a virtual Rupay card. To keep it real, you won't earn gold on rent, fuel, utilities, or international transactions—but you do get a 1% fuel surcharge waiver up to ₹500 every month. If you're ready to stop collecting useless points and start stacking actual gold, check it out here: 👇 🔗 https://link.uni.cards/r/bE8I80 #Collab #UniGoldX #DigitalGold #CreditCardRewards #PersonalFinance #LifetimeFreeCard

items.title: Not sure if SmartBuy has shifted to some other planet 🤔🌍. It’s been 12 days now, but their promised “3–4 days” are still not over. 😅 👉Amazon Pay Gift Cards are available on Gyftr’s own portal, but they’re missing from SmartBuy’s Gyftr for last 12 days. items.title: ⚠️ To all the people who are considering HSBC Demat account route to maintain TRV for Premier Account….. HSBC Demat Account, in its current form, cannot be a substitute for your regular demat account (like Zerodha), because HSBC currently does not offer an associated trading account. 👉 This means that once you transfer your Stocks or ETFs to your HSBC Demat Account, you cannot buy or sell them online like you do with a regular broker. ⏳ Selling a stock or ETF, which usually takes just a few seconds through Zerodha or any other trading platform, can take 5–7 days with HSBC Demat due to the offline process. There are only two ways to transact: 📌 Option 1: Contact your HSBC Relationship Manager (RM) and place an offline buy/sell request. 📌 Option 2: Transfer your Stocks/ETFs back from HSBC Demat to your regular demat account (like Zerodha). For this, you’ll need to fill the physical Delivery Instruction Slip (DIS), courier or hand it over to your RM, who will then initiate the transfer. ⏳ Both processes generally take around 5–7 days to complete. 💡 Earlier, HSBC had a tie-up with ICICI Securities, which allowed customers to buy and sell Stocks/ETFs instantly through an online trading account. That partnership ended last year. HSBC representatives have informed that they are working on bringing back an online trading facility, but there is no confirmed timeline yet. 📈 Most people (including myself) who have chosen the Demat route to maintain the Total Relationship Value (TRV) for the HSBC Premier Account have transferred only that portion of their Stocks/ETF portfolio which they do not intend to sell in the near future and are comfortable keeping locked in an account where selling may take 5–7 days. So, please understand these operational limitations before transferring your investments. Make an informed decision based on your own requirements, and if needed, consult your financial advisor before taking any such step. 📘 Visit SpendWisely Blog: Detailed credit card reviews & guides 👉 https://spendwisely.in Some links may be referral links in the above post. It supports the page without any cost to you. #creditcard #spendwisely #creditcard

items.title: Despite so many talk about HDFC Diners Club Black Metal (DCBM) Credit Card, its still an underrrated card. The DCBM Card delivers an impressive 5.83% effective reward rate on almost all regular online & offline spends (excluding the usual exclusions like fuel, rent, government payments, wallets, etc.). 💳 Here’s how: 👉 Base Rewards: 3.33% (5 Reward Points per ₹150 spent) 👉 Quarterly Milestone: +2.5% (10,000 Bonus Reward Points on ₹4 lakh quarterly spend) ✅ Total Effective Return: 5.83% on general spending. That’s an exceptional baseline reward rate for a premium credit card across everyday spending categories. On top of it, you get… 👇 🔥 Accelerated Reward Rates: 🏨 33.33% on Hotels, Jockey & PharmEasy ✈️ 16.66% on Flights, Bus Bookings & Myntra 🎁 10% on Gift Vouchers (including useful ones like Swiggy, Uber, etc.) To further increase reward rate, transfer the Reward Points at 1:1 to airline partners and redeem them strategically. ✈️ Flying Blue (Air France/KLM) – Redeem for Air France, KLM, Vietnam Airlines, Oman Air, Etihad, Saudia, Kenya Airways, Virgin Atlantic & more in one single airmile program. ✈️ Finnair Plus (Avios) – Transfer 1:1 to Finnair and then move Avios 1:1 to British Airways, Qatar Airways, Iberia, Aer Lingus. Great for flights on Qatar Airways, British Airways, Cathay Pacific, SriLankan Airlines, Iberia, Aer Lingus, Finnair & more. ✈️ Singapore Airlines KrisFlyer – Excellent for Singapore Airlines flights and Star Alliance redemptions across Asia-Pacific, Australia and beyond. ⚠️Acceptance Issue: Diners Club network’s acceptance issue is not as big as Amex and it has improved a lot in last few years. In fact, it’s almost negligible in metros and online spend. For anyone who spends consistently and knows how to redeem airline miles, DCBM remains one of the strongest all-round premium credit cards available today. 💯 📘 Visit SpendWisely Blog: Detailed credit card reviews & guides 👉 https://spendwisely.in Some links may be referral links in the above post. It supports the page without any cost to you. #creditcard #spendwisely #creditcard

items.title: Guide: Booking the Best Room with Marriott Bonvoy Points
items.snippet: Generally booking a hotel award night, which is doing so using points, gets you the base room. Some hotel loyalty programs, such as World of Hyatt, let you book suites or rooms with access to the club lounge and have a separate award chart. Not, however, Marriott Bonvoy.
